coreJobStatement: Manufacture rotating electrical machines that reliably convert between mechanical and electrical energy at specified power, voltage, frequency, and efficiency ratings
outputTypes: Large turbo-generator (>=100 MW) — steam or gas turbine driven; hydrogen or water cooled; 12-72 keybars per machine
Hydro-generator — slow-speed salient-pole; large stator diameter; high keybar count
Industrial motor (1-100 MW) — multi-purpose drive; standardised frame sizes
Medium generator (<100 MW) — gas/diesel driven; smaller keybar cross-section
Small / fractional motor (<1 MW) — automated coil insertion; keybars not applicable
hierarchy: Product VN (L7 through L4) — what process steps and equipment compose the electrical machine manufacturing chain
marketSize: Global electric generator market ~$31.7B (2024); ~$57.9B by 2034 (CAGR 6.2%) [SRC: web_search]

l6Systems.name: Lamination Fabrication
l6Systems.jobFamily: Stamp silicon steel lamination segments to specified geometry and insulation coating

l6Systems.name: Stator Core Assembly
l6Systems.jobFamily: Stack lamination segments onto structural keybar frame and compress to specified core density

l6Systems.name: Coil & Bar Winding
l6Systems.jobFamily: Wind and form electrical conductor bars/coils to stator slot geometry and insulation spec

l6Systems.name: Insulation & Impregnation
l6Systems.jobFamily: Impregnate wound stator with resin system to achieve required electrical isolation and thermal conductivity

l6Systems.name: Rotor Assembly
l6Systems.jobFamily: Assemble rotor core, field winding/magnets, and shaft into balanced rotating subassembly

l6Systems.name: Final Assembly & Integration
l6Systems.jobFamily: Integrate stator, rotor, bearings, and enclosure into complete rotating machine assembly

l6Systems.name: Factory Testing & Certification
l6Systems.jobFamily: Verify electrical, mechanical, and safety performance of completed machine to OEM and customer specification

l6Systems.name: Design & Simulation Engineering
l6Systems.jobFamily: Electromagnetic, thermal, and mechanical design governs all core steps

l6Systems.name: Quality & Dimensional Inspection
l6Systems.jobFamily: CMM, hardness testing, insulation resistance, and dimensional audit at every process gate

vnUnits.name: Stator Structural Frame
vnUnits.functionalJob: Precision steel skeleton onto which silicon steel lamination segments are stacked. Laminations interlock with keybars via dovetail/T-slot mating geometry. ZOLLERN PRIMARY POSITION at L4 (keybar profiles) within this L5.

vnUnits.name: Core Stacking & Pressing Equipment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Hydraulic core press (OEM-proprietary, ~90% share) for compressing lamination stacks to specified core density.

vnUnits.name: Core Testing Equipment
vnUnits.functionalJob: El-CID (electromagnetic core imperfection detection, ~60%) and ring flux test (~35%) for stator core quality verification.

vnUnits.name: Lamination Stamping Systems
vnUnits.functionalJob: Progressive die stamping (automated strip feed, ~70%) for silicon steel lamination segment production.

vnUnits.name: Bar Winding Machines
vnUnits.functionalJob: Semi-automated bar forming machine (~55%) for stator conductor bar winding.

vnUnits.name: Vacuum Pressure Impregnation (VPI) System
vnUnits.functionalJob: Impregnation of wound stator with epoxy resin under vacuum to achieve Class F/H insulation.

strategicPosition: ZOLLERN holds position at L4 (Keybar Profiles) within L5 (Stator Structural Frame) under L6b (Stator Core Assembly). Cold-drawn near-net-shape dovetail/T-slot profiles at IT8 tolerance. ~20% share of keybar supply. Competing against CNC-machined from bar (~65%), catalog profiles (~10%), and welded/fabricated (~5%).

sources.label: Global Electric Generator Market — Market Reports
